rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new architecture.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and resolve the first invalid identifier or helper on the same line.
If diagnostics point to implementation-level references in a component type annex, move valid bindings to the implementation annex or delete invalid guarantees.
generic_rules: Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and use only declared or imported identifiers.
Component type contracts should reference only features visible at that scope; implementation-level bindings belong in implementation annexes.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers unless they are present in the model's configured dependencies.
When resolving 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors, prioritize using existing visible identifiers over adding new declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level assignments must be placed in component implementation annexes.
Helper functions and constants must be declared before use and match the expected types.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture or assume new identifiers not present in the visible component scope.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by changing the expression type without aligning the helper declaration.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
